completedSteps.title: Create frontend folder
completedSteps.summary: Created the public-page folder for the standalone public frontend.

completedSteps.title: Create page file
completedSteps.summary: Created public-page/index.html with a valid HTML document shell.

completedSteps.title: Use standalone static frontend
completedSteps.summary: Implemented the page using plain HTML, CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript with no frontend framework.

completedSteps.title: Apply visual design
completedSteps.summary: Added a dark background, minimal typography, and a single centered card layout with responsive mobile behavior.

completedSteps.title: Add UI elements
completedSteps.summary: Added the page title, full-width URL input, Shorten button, hidden result area, clickable short URL, Copy button, and inline error message area.

completedSteps.title: Add API configuration
completedSteps.summary: Defined API_BASE as http://localhost:3000 and used it for the frontend fetch call.

completedSteps.title: Implement submit behavior
completedSteps.summary: Added Shorten button and Enter key submission, client-side http/https validation, inline validation errors, and POST /shorten with JSON body and Content-Type header.

completedSteps.title: Handle API response
completedSteps.summary: Handled successful API responses by showing the returned shortUrl as a clickable link, and handled failed responses by showing API error messages when available.

completedSteps.title: Implement copy behavior
completedSteps.summary: Added clipboard copy behavior for the generated short URL and changed the Copy button label to Copied! after copying.

verification: Confirmed backend exposes POST /shorten in backend/routes/public.js.
Confirmed createShortUrl returns shortUrl and shortCode in backend/controllers/publicController.js.
Confirmed cors() is enabled in backend/server.js.
Reviewed public-page/index.html after each implementation step.

remainingSteps.title: Ensure backend dependencies exist
remainingSteps.summary: Backend requirements were checked during the session, but this step has not been separately finalized as the next planned step.
